test.name: Project-status request should not trigger research-exhaustiveness
test.description: User asks for an overall project status update. That's project-status — research-exhaustiveness evaluates whether research on a specific question is reasonably exhaustive, not the project's overall state. The boundary matters because both skills sit near the 'how are things going?' decision point, but project-status reports across all questions while research-exhaustiveness writes the exhaustive_declaration for one specific question.

input.user_message: Give me an overall status update on this research project — where do we stand across all questions?

negative.explanation: The user is asking for a project-wide status report, not an exhaustiveness evaluation of a specific question. project-status summarizes progress across all questions; research-exhaustiveness writes the exhaustive_declaration for one question. The request mentions no specific question and asks for a broad overview.

judge_context: Should route to project-status rather than evaluating exhaustiveness or writing a declaration for any specific question
